name: Gello
description: Gello was a female demon or ghost in Greek mythology, notorious for stealing newborn babies and causing infant mortality. She was often depicted as a vengeful spirit, driven by the loss of her own children. Accounts vary, but generally, Gello was once a mortal woman who suffered the tragic death of her offspring, leading to her transformation into a malevolent being consumed by envy and a desire to inflict similar pain on other mothers. She was believed to sneak into homes under the cover of darkness, preying on vulnerable infants. Protective measures, such as placing amulets, writing protective spells on the walls, and invoking the names of protective deities like Hecate and Lamia's counterpart, were employed to ward off Gello's evil influence. The Gello myth served as a way to explain the then-unexplainable phenomenon of sudden infant death syndrome and maternal grief, highlighting societal anxieties surrounding childbirth and the fragility of life. The exact origins and details of Gello's story differ across various regions and time periods within ancient Greece, but her role as a child-snatching demon remained consistent. She is a figure of fear and a cautionary tale, embodying the dangers lurking in the shadows and the profound loss experienced by mothers.
subtitle: Infant-snatching demon of Greek myth
appearance: Gello's appearance is often described as monstrous and terrifying, though she can shapeshift to appear as a seemingly normal woman to lure victims. She is depicted with sharp claws, wild hair, and a gaunt, pale face. Some accounts portray her with serpent-like features or grotesque deformities, reflecting her corrupted and vengeful nature.
habitat: Gello lurks in dark and isolated places, such as abandoned homes, graveyards, and the outskirts of towns. She is most active at night, seeking out vulnerable infants in unguarded homes.
behavior: Gello is driven by envy and a desire to inflict pain on mothers. She is cunning and stealthy, using deception to gain access to her victims. She is relentless in her pursuit and takes pleasure in causing suffering. Her behavior is motivated by the trauma of her own loss, twisted into a malevolent force.

keyMyths.title: Gello's Origins
keyMyths.description: Gello was once a mortal woman who lost her own children, leading to her transformation into a child-snatching demon. This loss fueled her envy and drove her to seek vengeance on other mothers by stealing their newborns. Her story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of unchecked grief and the potential for tragedy to warp the human spirit.

sources.author: Jane Ellen Harrison
sources.work: Prolegomena to the Study of Greek Religion
sources.description: Discusses the role of female demons and their connection to fertility and childbirth rituals.
sources.text: Discusses the role of female demons and their connection to fertility and childbirth rituals.
